task_completion.description: Open the required sample scene in OpenToonz
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open and the title bar, xsheet, or project browser indicates that 'dwanko_run.tnz' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Character positioned on the left side at frame 1
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz timeline or xsheet is on frame 1, and the character is positioned on the left side of the canvas/viewer, near the ground level.

task_completion.description: Character positioned at the peak of the jump at frame 12
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz timeline or xsheet is on frame 12, and the character is positioned in the center of the canvas horizontally and elevated near the top (the peak of the parabolic arc).

task_completion.description: Character positioned on the right side at frame 24
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz timeline or xsheet is on frame 24, and the character is positioned on the right side of the canvas/viewer, back near the ground level.

task_completion.description: Configure render settings for PNG sequence with transparent background
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window in OpenToonz is visible, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/jump_arc/' (or similar), the file format set to 'png', and settings indicating an Alpha channel (transparent background) is enabled.
A file manager or terminal window shows a sequence of at least 24 PNG files (e.g., ending in 0001.png to 0024.png) successfully created in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/jump_arc/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to perform the animation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenToonz interface (tools, xsheet, viewer) rather than just terminal commands or external scripts.

integrity.description: The animation was created using keyframes or interpolation within the software
integrity.visual_evidence: Keyframe indicators, motion paths, or interpolation settings are visible in the OpenToonz xsheet, timeline, or viewer, proving the arc was animated.

integrity.description: The output files were rendered directly from OpenToonz
integrity.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar or the OpenToonz render window is visible, showing the frames being generated, rather than files being copied from elsewhere.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is verified as '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The output directory must be '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/jump_arc'. The animation must contain at least 24 frames. To verify the parabolic arc, specifically check the character's position at frame 1 (start, left side), frame 12 (peak, center), and frame 24 (end, right side).
